error_signature: capitalize formula breaks when condition is true
title: Capitalize formula fails with boolean variable true
symptom: The capitalize formula works when the boolean variable is false but fails when it is true, leading to unexpected behavior.
root_cause: The capitalize formula evaluates all parameters, and if the boolean variable is true, it may not be treated as a string, causing the formula to break. This is due to the way the formula interprets non-string values.
fix_steps: Modify the capitalize formula to include a default value, e.g., `capitalize(yourvariable || '')`
Test the formula with the boolean variable set to true to ensure it works as expected.
agent_instruction: Update the capitalize formula to handle non-string values by adding a default value.
